norwegian: Forskningsresultatene tyder på at sammenhengen mellom søvn og hukommelse er sterkere enn tidligere antatt.
english: The research results suggest that the connection between sleep and memory is stronger than previously assumed.
notes: Academic hedging: 'tyder på at' (suggests that)
accepted_answers: The research findings suggest that the connection between sleep and memory is stronger than previously assumed

norwegian: Det kan argumenteres for at klimapolitikken ikke har hatt tilstrekkelig effekt på utslippsnivåene.
english: It can be argued that climate policy has not had a sufficient effect on emission levels.
notes: Impersonal argumentative construction: 'det kan argumenteres for at'
accepted_answers: It can be argued that climate policy has not had a sufficient impact on emission levels
It can be argued that climate policy has not had an adequate effect on emission levels

norwegian: Ifølge flere studier ___ digitale hjelpemidler læringsutbyttet i grunnskolen.
english: According to several studies, digital tools improve learning outcomes in primary school.
notes: forbedrer

norwegian: Det er verdt å merke seg at resultatene fra denne undersøkelsen ikke uten videre kan generaliseres.
english: It is worth noting that the results from this survey cannot straightforwardly be generalized.
notes: Hedging phrase: 'ikke uten videre' (not straightforwardly/automatically)
accepted_answers: It is worth noting that the results of this survey cannot straightforwardly be generalized

norwegian: Som nevnt i innledningen, bygger denne analysen på et begrenset datamateriale.
english: As mentioned in the introduction, this analysis is based on a limited data set.
notes: Intertextual reference phrase; V2 after fronted adverbial clause

norwegian: Funnene i denne rapporten ___ tidligere forskning på feltet.
english: The findings in this report support earlier research in the field.
notes: støtter

norwegian: Det foreligger lite forskning på hvordan sosiale medier påvirker politisk deltakelse blant unge voksne.
english: There is little research on how social media affects political participation among young adults.
notes: 'det foreligger' is a formal existential construction meaning 'there exists/there is available'
accepted_answers: There is limited research on how social media affects political participation among young adults

norwegian: Hensikten med denne artikkelen er å belyse de viktigste utfordringene knyttet til digital personvern.
english: The purpose of this article is to shed light on the most important challenges related to digital privacy.
notes: 'hensikten med ... er å' (the purpose of ... is to) — formal purpose framing

norwegian: Det er rimelig å anta at befolkningsveksten vil fortsette å legge press på boligmarkedet.
english: It is reasonable to assume that population growth will continue to put pressure on the housing market.
notes: 'det er rimelig å anta at' — formal hedging, reasonable assumption marker

norwegian: Teorien ___ ikke av alle forskere, og det finnes alternative forklaringsmodeller.
english: The theory is not accepted by all researchers, and there are alternative explanatory models.
notes: aksepteres

norwegian: På bakgrunn av dette kan vi ikke trekke noen endelige konklusjoner uten ytterligere undersøkelser.
english: On this basis, we cannot draw any definitive conclusions without further investigation.
notes: V2 after 'på bakgrunn av dette'; formal hedging on conclusions

norwegian: Studien antyder at det er en signifikant korrelasjon mellom stress og søvnkvalitet hos studenter.
english: The study suggests that there is a significant correlation between stress and sleep quality in students.
notes: 'antyder at' (suggests that) — softer claim verb than 'viser'

norwegian: I lys av disse funnene ___ det nødvendig å revidere den eksisterende politikken.
english: In light of these findings, it seems necessary to revise the existing policy.
notes: synes

norwegian: Denne tilnærmingen har vist seg å være mer effektiv enn tradisjonelle metoder i flere kontrollerte forsøk.
english: This approach has proven to be more effective than traditional methods in several controlled trials.
notes: 'har vist seg å være' — reflexive evidential construction for proven results
accepted_answers: This approach has been shown to be more effective than traditional methods in several controlled trials

norwegian: Det gjenstår å se om disse tiltakene vil ha den ønskede effekten på lang sikt.
english: It remains to be seen whether these measures will have the desired effect in the long term.
notes: 'det gjenstår å se om' — formal expression of uncertainty about future outcomes
accepted_answers: It remains to be seen whether these measures will have the desired effect in the long run
It remains to be seen if these measures will have the desired effect in the long term

norwegian: Resultatene er i tråd med hypotesen om at økt fysisk aktivitet reduserer symptomer på depresjon.
english: The results are in line with the hypothesis that increased physical activity reduces symptoms of depression.
notes: 'i tråd med' (in line with) — formal alignment phrase

norwegian: Det bør understrekes at utvalget i denne studien ikke er representativt for hele befolkningen.
english: It should be emphasised that the sample in this study is not representative of the entire population.
notes: 'det bør understrekes at' — formal caveat phrase

norwegian: Metoden som ble benyttet, ___ i detalj i metodedelen av rapporten.
english: The method that was used is described in detail in the methodology section of the report.
notes: beskrives

norwegian: Denne studien bidrar til å fylle et gap i litteraturen ved å undersøke faktorer som tidligere er oversett.
english: This study helps to fill a gap in the literature by examining factors that have previously been overlooked.
notes: 'bidrar til å fylle et gap i litteraturen' — standard academic contribution framing

norwegian: Basert på de foreliggende dataene ___ det vanskelig å fastslå årsaksforholdet med sikkerhet.
english: Based on the available data, it is difficult to establish the causal relationship with certainty.
notes: er

norwegian: Selv om dataene er begrenset, gir de likevel et nyttig innblikk i fenomenet.
english: Although the data is limited, it still provides a useful insight into the phenomenon.
notes: V2 after 'selv om'-clause; 'likevel' as concessive adverb in main clause

norwegian: Det er all grunn til å tro at videre forskning vil avdekke nye aspekter ved dette spørsmålet.
english: There is every reason to believe that further research will uncover new aspects of this question.
notes: 'det er all grunn til å tro at' — strong academic hedging phrase
accepted_answers: There is every reason to believe that further research will reveal new aspects of this question

norwegian: Analysen avslører et komplekst mønster av faktorer som samvirker på uforutsigbare måter.
english: The analysis reveals a complex pattern of factors that interact in unpredictable ways.
notes: 'samvirker' (interact/work together) — formal academic verb

norwegian: Funnene ___ at en mer helhetlig tilnærming er nødvendig for å løse problemet effektivt.
english: The findings indicate that a more holistic approach is necessary to solve the problem effectively.
notes: indikerer

norwegian: Spørsmålet om hvorvidt teknologi kan erstatte menneskelig kontakt er fortsatt omdiskutert.
english: The question of whether technology can replace human contact remains contested.
notes: 'hvorvidt' (whether) — formal conjunction used in academic writing
accepted_answers: The question of whether technology can replace human contact remains debated
The question of whether technology can replace human contact is still contested

norwegian: Denne artikkelen tar sikte på å gi en oversikt over de viktigste teoriene innen feltet.
english: This article aims to provide an overview of the most important theories within the field.
notes: 'tar sikte på å' (aims to) — formal purpose verb phrase

norwegian: I motsetning til tidligere studier ___ denne undersøkelsen også de langsiktige konsekvensene.
english: In contrast to earlier studies, this investigation also examines the long-term consequences.
notes: undersøker

norwegian: Det kan hevdes at den eksisterende lovgivningen ikke er tilpasset de nye teknologiske realitetene.
english: It can be maintained that the existing legislation is not adapted to the new technological realities.
notes: 'det kan hevdes at' — impersonal argumentative construction

norwegian: Disse funnene reiser viktige spørsmål om etikken rundt bruk av kunstig intelligens i helsesektoren.
english: These findings raise important questions about the ethics of using artificial intelligence in the health sector.
notes: 'reiser viktige spørsmål om' — academic framing for raising issues

norwegian: Konklusjonen er at ytterligere empirisk forskning er påkrevd før politiske tiltak kan iverksettes.
english: The conclusion is that further empirical research is required before policy measures can be implemented.
notes: 'er påkrevd' — formal passive expressing necessity; 'iverksettes' (implemented) — formal passive infinitive
accepted_answers: The conclusion is that further empirical research is needed before policy measures can be implemented

norwegian: Kunne De være så vennlig å sende meg den aktuelle rapporten så snart det lar seg gjøre?
english: Could you be so kind as to send me the relevant report as soon as possible?
notes: Formal 'De/Dem' address; 'så snart det lar seg gjøre' = as soon as it can be arranged

norwegian: Hei! Skal vi ikke bare ta en kaffe og prate litt? Det er lenge siden sist.
english: Hey! Shall we not just grab a coffee and chat a bit? It's been a long time.
notes: Informal register: 'Hei', 'bare', 'prate litt', 'lenge siden sist' — all informal markers
accepted_answers: Hey! Shall we not just have a coffee and chat a bit? It's been a long time

norwegian: Jeg tillater meg å henvende meg til Dem angående den forestående kontraktsforhandlingen.
english: I take the liberty of contacting you regarding the upcoming contract negotiation.
notes: 'tillater meg å henvende meg' — very formal self-deprecating introductory phrase; 'Dem' formal address
accepted_answers: I take the liberty of reaching out to you regarding the upcoming contract negotiation

norwegian: I møtet i dag ___ det diskutert flere forslag til effektivisering av arbeidsprosessene.
english: In today's meeting, several proposals for streamlining work processes were discussed.
notes: ble

norwegian: Vet du hva, jeg skjønte egentlig ikke hva han mente, men jeg lot som om jeg gjorde.
english: You know what, I didn't really understand what he meant, but I pretended I did.
notes: Informal: 'vet du hva', 'egentlig', 'lot som om' — conversational filler and expression
accepted_answers: You know what, I didn't really understand what he meant, but I pretended to

norwegian: Vi ber om unnskyldning for eventuelle ulemper dette måtte medføre.
english: We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.
notes: 'ber om unnskyldning for' — formal apology; 'eventuelle' = any/possible (formal hedging adjective)

norwegian: Herr direktør, ___ jeg få lov til å presentere våre foreløpige resultater?
english: Mr. Director, may I have permission to present our preliminary results?
notes: måtte
accepted_answers: ville

norwegian: Det er utrolig kult at du fikk den jobben! Når begynner du?
english: It's incredibly cool that you got that job! When do you start?
notes: Informal: 'utrolig kult' — would be completely inappropriate in formal context
accepted_answers: That's incredibly cool that you got that job! When do you start

norwegian: Selskapet ønsker å uttrykke sin dypeste takknemlighet for Deres mangeårige og trofaste tjeneste.
english: The company wishes to express its deepest gratitude for your many years of faithful service.
notes: 'Deres' — formal possessive; 'trofaste tjeneste' — formal, somewhat archaic

norwegian: Æsj, den filmen var jo helt håpløs. Vi burde ha valgt noe annet.
english: Ugh, that film was absolutely hopeless. We should have chosen something else.
notes: 'Æsj' — informal exclamation; 'jo' — informal confirmation particle; 'håpløs' colloquially meaning terrible
accepted_answers: Ugh, that movie was absolutely hopeless. We should have chosen something else
Ugh, that film was absolutely hopeless. We should have picked something else

norwegian: Med henvisning til Deres brev av 15. mai ønsker vi å klargjøre vår posisjon i saken.
english: With reference to your letter of 15 May, we wish to clarify our position in the matter.
notes: 'Med henvisning til' — standard formal business letter opening phrase

norwegian: Fikk du sett den kampen i går? Den var helt rå, altså!
english: Did you catch that match yesterday? It was absolutely brilliant!
notes: 'rå' (raw/brutal) — informal slang for impressive; 'altså' — informal emphasis particle
accepted_answers: Did you see that match yesterday? It was absolutely brilliant
Did you watch that match yesterday? It was absolutely brilliant

norwegian: Styret ___ enstemmig vedtatt å utsette avgjørelsen til neste kvartal.
english: The board has unanimously decided to postpone the decision until next quarter.
notes: har

norwegian: Jeg har ikke peiling på hva som foregår. Kan du ikke bare forklare det ordentlig?
english: I have no idea what's going on. Can you not just explain it properly?
notes: 'ikke peiling' — informal idiom for having no idea; 'bare' and 'ordentlig' are informal intensifiers
accepted_answers: I have no idea what's going on. Can't you just explain it properly

norwegian: Undertegnede ønsker herved å søke om permisjon fra sin stilling i perioden 1.–30. juni.
english: The undersigned hereby wishes to apply for leave of absence from their position during the period 1–30 June.
notes: 'Undertegnede' — formal self-reference (the undersigned); 'herved' — formal adverb (hereby)
accepted_answers: The undersigned hereby wishes to apply for leave from their position during the period 1–30 June

norwegian: Gitt selskapets finansielle situasjon ___ det nødvendig å foreta umiddelbare tiltak.
english: Given the company's financial situation, it is necessary to take immediate action.
notes: er

norwegian: Det var ikke meningen å støte deg, men jeg synes ærlig talt at du overreagerte litt.
english: It wasn't meant to offend you, but honestly I think you overreacted a little.
notes: Semi-formal/neutral spoken register; 'ærlig talt' (honestly speaking) and 'litt' soften the criticism

norwegian: Vi ser frem til et fortsatt godt samarbeid og ønsker Dem alt godt i Deres nye stilling.
english: We look forward to a continued good working relationship and wish you all the best in your new position.
notes: Formal closing phrase; 'Dem'/'Deres' — formal address throughout

norwegian: Du, jeg måtte bare si at du virkelig er flink til å håndtere slike situasjoner.
english: Hey, I just had to say that you are really good at handling those kinds of situations.
notes: Conversational 'du' as attention-getter; 'bare' as informal softener; 'flink til' — informal praise
accepted_answers: Hi, I just had to say that you are really good at handling those kinds of situations
Hey, I just had to say that you are really good at handling that kind of situation

norwegian: Departementet ___ at søknadene vil bli behandlet innen to uker fra mottaksdato.
english: The ministry informs that applications will be processed within two weeks from the date of receipt.
notes: opplyser

norwegian: Sjefen min sa «god jobb» til meg i dag. Ikke dårlig med tanke på at det har vært en tøff uke.
english: My boss said 'good job' to me today. Not bad considering it has been a tough week.
notes: Neutral/informal spoken register about a workplace context; contrast with formal workplace writing

norwegian: Vi gjør oppmerksom på at betalingsfristen for den aktuelle fakturaen nå er overskredet.
english: We would like to draw your attention to the fact that the payment deadline for the relevant invoice has now been exceeded.
notes: 'gjør oppmerksom på' — formal phrase for drawing attention; 'den aktuelle' = the relevant (formal adjective)

norwegian: Etter en grundig ___ av alternativene anbefaler komitéen å velge det andre forslaget.
english: After a thorough assessment of the alternatives, the committee recommends choosing the second proposal.
notes: vurdering

norwegian: Kom igjen, ta det med ro. Det ordner seg sikkert, du skal se.
english: Come on, take it easy. It'll probably work out, you'll see.
notes: Informal reassurance: 'kom igjen', 'ta det med ro', 'ordner seg' — all informal expressions
accepted_answers: Come on, relax. It'll probably work out, you'll see

norwegian: Forespørselen Deres er mottatt og vil bli behandlet av ansvarlig saksbehandler.
english: Your enquiry has been received and will be processed by the responsible case handler.
notes: 'Forespørselen Deres' fronted; 'saksbehandler' — formal bureaucratic job title

norwegian: Vi setter stor pris på Deres ___ og gleder oss til å møte Dem personlig.
english: We greatly appreciate your patience and look forward to meeting you in person.
notes: tålmodighet

norwegian: Orker du ikke gidde å svare på meldingene mine? Det er litt ugreit.
english: Can't you be bothered to reply to my messages? That's a bit off.
notes: 'orker ikke gidde' — double low-energy informal verbs; 'ugreit' — informal for not OK/awkward
accepted_answers: Can't you be bothered to respond to my messages? That's a bit off

norwegian: Vedtaket ble fattet med seks stemmer mot to, og vil tre i kraft fra og med neste regnskapsår.
english: The decision was made with six votes to two, and will come into effect from the next financial year.
notes: 'vedtaket ble fattet' — formal passive; 'tre i kraft' (come into force) — formal legal phrase
accepted_answers: The decision was made by six votes to two, and will come into effect from the next financial year
The decision was passed with six votes to two, and will come into effect from the next financial year

norwegian: Takk for sist, forresten! Vi må gjøre det igjen snart.
english: Thanks for last time, by the way! We must do it again soon.
notes: 'takk for sist' — uniquely Norwegian social phrase thanking someone for a previous meeting

norwegian: Konferansen ___ avholdt på Radisson Blu Hotel, og samtlige presentasjoner vil bli strømmet direkte.
english: The conference will be held at Radisson Blu Hotel, and all presentations will be streamed live.
notes: vil bli

norwegian: Bilen ble reparert av en erfaren mekaniker på verkstedet i nærheten.
english: The car was repaired by an experienced mechanic at the nearby workshop.
notes: 'ble reparert av' — agentive passive with 'av' + agent

norwegian: Rapporten er allerede levert, men tilbakemeldingene er ikke mottatt ennå.
english: The report has already been submitted, but the feedback has not been received yet.
notes: Stative passive: 'er levert' / 'er mottatt' — state resulting from action, not the action itself
accepted_answers: The report has already been delivered, but the feedback has not been received yet
The report has already been submitted, but the feedback has not yet been received

norwegian: Døren er ___ fra innsiden, og ingen kan komme ut.
english: The door is locked from the inside, and no one can get out.
notes: låst

norwegian: Det bygges flere nye leiligheter i sentrum for å møte den voksende etterspørselen.
english: Several new apartments are being built in the centre to meet growing demand.
notes: Impersonal s-passive: 'det bygges' — agentless, describes ongoing process
accepted_answers: Several new apartments are being built in the center to meet growing demand

norwegian: Alle dokumentene ble gjennomgått nøye av revisorene før rapporten ble godkjent.
english: All the documents were carefully reviewed by the auditors before the report was approved.
notes: Two passive constructions in sequence; 'av revisorene' — agentive phrase

norwegian: Resultatene ___ offentliggjort i neste ukes avis.
english: The results will be made public in next week's newspaper.
notes: vil bli

norwegian: I løpet av de siste årene er det gjort store fremskritt innen behandling av kreft.
english: Over the last few years, great advances have been made in the treatment of cancer.
notes: Impersonal passive: 'er det gjort' — fronted time adverbial triggers V2 with det-subject

norwegian: Pakken ble sendt mandag og burde allerede ___ mottatt.
english: The package was sent on Monday and should already have been received.
notes: ha blitt

norwegian: Vinduene er satt inn, gulvet er lagt, og huset er nesten ferdig.
english: The windows have been installed, the floor has been laid, and the house is almost finished.
notes: Three stative passives in sequence; all describe a resulting state
accepted_answers: The windows have been fitted, the floor has been laid, and the house is almost finished

norwegian: Det ble ikke tatt noen endelig beslutning i møtet, og saken utsettes til neste uke.
english: No final decision was taken in the meeting, and the matter is being postponed until next week.
notes: Impersonal passive: 'det ble tatt'; s-passive: 'utsettes' — both agentless
accepted_answers: No final decision was made in the meeting, and the matter is being postponed until next week

norwegian: Han ble bedt om å forlate møtet etter den høylytte diskusjonen.
english: He was asked to leave the meeting after the heated discussion.
notes: 'ble bedt om å' — passive with infinitive complement

norwegian: Boken er skrevet på en måte som gjør den tilgjengelig for et bredt publikum.
english: The book is written in a way that makes it accessible to a wide audience.
notes: Stative passive 'er skrevet' describes the book's condition/manner

norwegian: Det ___ ikke funnet noen feil i systemet etter den grundige gjennomgangen.
english: No faults were found in the system after the thorough review.
notes: ble

norwegian: Ny forskning viser at mange sykdommer kan forebygges dersom de oppdages tidlig nok.
english: New research shows that many diseases can be prevented if they are detected early enough.
notes: Modal s-passive: 'kan forebygges', 'oppdages' — agentless modal passives

norwegian: Prosjektet ble avsluttet i tide og er nå overlevert til kunden.
english: The project was completed on time and has now been handed over to the client.
notes: Dynamic passive 'ble avsluttet' vs. stative passive 'er overlevert' — contrast
accepted_answers: The project was finished on time and has now been handed over to the client
The project was completed on time and has now been delivered to the client

norwegian: Alle ble informert om endringen, men ikke alle hadde ___ e-posten.
english: Everyone was informed of the change, but not everyone had read the email.
notes: lest

norwegian: Det forventes at alle ansatte ___ for sertifiseringskurset innen utgangen av måneden.
english: It is expected that all employees register for the certification course by the end of the month.
notes: registrerer seg

norwegian: Husene som ble bygget på 1970-tallet er nå i ferd med å renoveres.
english: The houses that were built in the 1970s are now in the process of being renovated.
notes: 'i ferd med å renoveres' — progressive passive construction
accepted_answers: The houses built in the 1970s are now in the process of being renovated

norwegian: Kontrakten må signeres av begge parter før noen arbeid kan påbegynnes.
english: The contract must be signed by both parties before any work can be commenced.
notes: Modal passive: 'må signeres av'; s-passive: 'kan påbegynnes'

norwegian: Feilen ble oppdaget av en tilfeldig bruker og ___ straks videreformidlet til utviklingsteamet.
english: The error was discovered by a random user and was immediately passed on to the development team.
notes: ble

norwegian: Det antas at omtrent to millioner nordmenn er direkte berørt av de nye skattereglene.
english: It is assumed that approximately two million Norwegians are directly affected by the new tax rules.
notes: Impersonal passive 'det antas at'; stative passive 'er berørt'
accepted_answers: It is assumed that around two million Norwegians are directly affected by the new tax rules
It is estimated that approximately two million Norwegians are directly affected by the new tax rules

norwegian: Systemet er designet slik at feil automatisk ___ og loggføres.
english: The system is designed so that errors are automatically detected and logged.
notes: oppdages

norwegian: Landsbyen ble grunnlagt i middelalderen og er i dag et populært turistmål.
english: The village was founded in the Middle Ages and is today a popular tourist destination.
notes: Historical passive 'ble grunnlagt'; stative 'er' in present — temporal contrast

norwegian: Det sies at nordmenn er verdens lykkeligste folk, men det er ikke lett å måle lykke.
english: It is said that Norwegians are the world's happiest people, but it is not easy to measure happiness.
notes: S-passive 'det sies' — impersonal passive for common knowledge/hearsay
accepted_answers: It is said that Norwegians are the happiest people in the world, but it is not easy to measure happiness

norwegian: Vedlikeholdsarbeidet ___ gjennomført i løpet av natten for å unngå forstyrrelser i arbeidstiden.
english: The maintenance work will be carried out during the night to avoid disruptions during working hours.
notes: vil bli

norwegian: Samtlige forslag ble vurdert, men bare to av dem ble antatt til videre behandling.
english: All proposals were evaluated, but only two of them were accepted for further processing.
notes: Two passive clauses with 'samtlige' (formal for 'all') and agentless construction

norwegian: Loven ble vedtatt av Stortinget i 2019 og trådte i kraft fra 1. januar 2020.
english: The law was passed by the Storting in 2019 and came into force from 1 January 2020.
notes: 'ble vedtatt av' — agentive passive; 'trådte i kraft' — active idiom for coming into force
accepted_answers: The law was passed by the Storting in 2019 and came into effect from 1 January 2020
The law was enacted by the Storting in 2019 and came into force from 1 January 2020

norwegian: Det er ikke avklart hvem som skal ha ansvaret for å dekke de ekstra kostnadene.
english: It has not been clarified who is to be responsible for covering the extra costs.
notes: Stative passive 'er ikke avklart'; impersonal 'det' subject

norwegian: Funnene ble presentert på konferansen og ble positivt mottatt av fagmiljøet.
english: The findings were presented at the conference and were positively received by the research community.
notes: Coordinated passives; 'positivt mottatt' — adverb modifying passive past participle

norwegian: Prisene på boliger har blitt drevet opp av lav rente og høy etterspørsel de siste årene.
english: Housing prices have been driven up by low interest rates and high demand in recent years.
notes: Present perfect passive: 'har blitt drevet opp av' — with agentive 'av'-phrase

norwegian: Selv om han hadde forberedt seg grundig, følte han seg ikke trygg rett før presentasjonen.
english: Even though he had prepared thoroughly, he did not feel confident just before the presentation.
notes: 'selv om' — concessive subordinator; V2 in main clause after subordinate

norwegian: Til tross for at budsjettet ble kuttet, klarte teamet likevel å levere prosjektet i tide.
english: Despite the budget being cut, the team still managed to deliver the project on time.
notes: 'til tross for at' — concessive; 'likevel' (nevertheless) — concessive adverb in main clause
accepted_answers: Despite the budget cuts, the team still managed to deliver the project on time

norwegian: Mange støtter forslaget; ___ er det viktig å høre kritikerne også.
english: Many support the proposal; however, it is important to also listen to the critics.
notes: imidlertid

norwegian: Selv om teknologien finnes, er det ikke gitt at den vil bli tatt i bruk i stor skala.
english: Even though the technology exists, it is not guaranteed that it will be adopted on a large scale.
notes: 'ikke gitt at' — Norwegian idiom: not a given that; V2 after 'selv om'-clause
accepted_answers: Even though the technology exists, it is not certain that it will be adopted on a large scale

norwegian: Han er svært intelligent; ___ mangler han evnen til å samarbeide med andre.
english: He is very intelligent; nevertheless, he lacks the ability to collaborate with others.
notes: likevel

norwegian: Til tross for de mange utfordringene har regjeringen opprettholdt sin støtte til prosjektet.
english: Despite the many challenges, the government has maintained its support for the project.
notes: 'til tross for' + noun phrase (no 'at'); V2 inversion in main clause

norwegian: Forslaget er interessant; det er ___ for tidlig å si om det vil virke i praksis.

english: She remembered that the doctor had said that she should avoid strenuous activity for two weeks.
notes: burde

norwegian: Assistenten fortalte at hun hadde sendt rapporten, men at den hadde kommet i retur.
english: The assistant said that she had sent the report, but that it had come back.
notes: Past perfect backshift in coordinated reported clauses

norwegian: Undersøkelsen viste at de fleste respondentene mente at helsevesenet ___ forbedres betydelig.
english: The survey showed that most respondents felt that the healthcare system needed to be significantly improved.
notes: måtte

norwegian: Statsministeren uttalte at regjeringen hadde gjort alt som sto i dens makt for å håndtere krisen.
english: The Prime Minister stated that the government had done everything in its power to handle the crisis.
notes: 'uttalte at' — formal speech act verb; 'alt som sto i dens makt' — idiomatic
accepted_answers: The Prime Minister said that the government had done everything in its power to handle the crisis

norwegian: Hun innrømmet at hun ___ kjent til problemet lenge, men ikke sagt noe av frykt for konsekvensene.
english: She admitted that she had known about the problem for a long time, but had not said anything out of fear of the consequences.
notes: hadde

norwegian: Journalisten spurte om statsråden visste at rapporten hadde blitt holdt tilbake i flere måneder.
english: The journalist asked whether the minister knew that the report had been withheld for several months.
notes: Reported question with 'om'; past perfect passive in embedded clause
accepted_answers: The journalist asked if the minister knew that the report had been withheld for several months

norwegian: Kontrakten spesifiserte at alle betalinger ___ gjennomføres innen 30 dager etter fakturadato.
english: The contract specified that all payments were to be made within 30 days of the invoice date.
notes: skulle

norwegian: De bemerket at det hadde tatt mye lengre tid enn de hadde forventet, og beklaget forsinkelsen.
english: They noted that it had taken much longer than they had expected, and apologised for the delay.
notes: Double past perfect; comparative backshift: 'tar' → 'hadde tatt'

norwegian: Forskeren konkluderte med at hypotesen ___ bekreftet av dataene og at videre forskning var nødvendig.
english: The researcher concluded that the hypothesis had been confirmed by the data and that further research was necessary.
notes: var blitt

norwegian: Mannen forklarte at han ikke hadde ment å si noe støtende og bad om unnskyldning.
english: The man explained that he had not meant to say anything offensive and asked for forgiveness.
notes: Negative past perfect backshift; 'be om unnskyldning' — apologise

norwegian: Rapporten antydet at prisene ___ stige ytterligere i løpet av de neste månedene.

notes: ville

norwegian: Han erkjente at han hadde tatt feil og at han burde ha lyttet til rådene han fikk.
english: He acknowledged that he had been wrong and that he should have listened to the advice he was given.
notes: 'erkjente at' — acknowledge; 'burde ha lyttet' — modal perfect in backshifted clause
accepted_answers: He admitted that he had been wrong and that he should have listened to the advice he was given
He acknowledged he had been wrong and that he should have listened to the advice he was given

norwegian: Om bare jeg hadde visst det tidligere, ville jeg ha handlet helt annerledes.

norwegian: Hvis jeg var deg, ville jeg snakket med legen så snart som mulig.

accepted_answers: If I were you, I would speak to the doctor as soon as possible

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske at jeg ___ flytte til et varmere land.
english: I wish that I could move to a warmer country.
notes: kunne

norwegian: Om bare det ikke hadde regnet den dagen, ville vi ha kunnet gjennomføre konserten utendørs.
english: If only it had not rained that day, we would have been able to hold the concert outdoors.

norwegian: Hadde de ___ bedre forberedt, ville utfallet trolig blitt annerledes.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske at vi ___ kjent hverandre bedre da vi var unge.

english: I wish she were still here — there is so much I would have liked to say to her.

I wish she were still here — there is so much I would have liked to tell her

The project was completed on time and within budget. Furthermore, it exceeded the expectations of all involved parties

The project had three main aims. The first was achieved, the second partially, and the third not at all

norwegian: Den første løsningen er rask, men kostbar. ___ er den andre løsningen billigere, men tar mer tid.

A well-written letter can open many doors in one's professional life

english: The report contains many surprising findings that challenge existing theories.

norwegian: En stadig voksende andel av befolkningen arbeider hjemmefra minst én dag i uken.
english: An ever-growing proportion of the population works from home at least one day a week.
notes: 'stadig voksende' — adverb + present participle as compound attributive
accepted_answers: An ever-increasing proportion of the population works from home at least one day a week

norwegian: De ___ ressursene må fordeles rettferdig mellom alle involverte parter.
english: The limited resources must be distributed fairly between all involved parties.
notes: begrensede

norwegian: Hvem ville vel trodd at en liten oppstart fra Bergen skulle bli et globalt selskap?
english: Who would have thought that a small start-up from Bergen would become a global company?
notes: Rhetorical question: 'hvem ville vel trodd at'; 'vel' adds rhetorical softening
accepted_answers: Who would have thought that a small startup from Bergen would become a global company

norwegian: Resultatet — som mange hadde fryktet — ble langt verre enn noen hadde forventet.
english: The result — which many had feared — turned out to be far worse than anyone had expected.
notes: Parenthetical insertion with dashes: 'som mange hadde fryktet' interrupts main clause
accepted_answers: The outcome — which many had feared — turned out to be far worse than anyone had expected
The result — which many had feared — proved to be far worse than anyone had expected

norwegian: Hvordan kan vi forvente at barn skal lære å lese når de bruker så mye tid foran skjermer?
english: How can we expect children to learn to read when they spend so much time in front of screens?
notes: Rhetorical question implying a negative answer; V2 after interrogative fronting

norwegian: Prosjektet — etter måneder med forsinkelser og budsjettoverskridelser — ble endelig ferdigstilt.
english: The project — after months of delays and budget overruns — was finally completed.
notes: Long parenthetical with dashes between subject and predicate
accepted_answers: The project — after months of delays and budget overruns — was finally finished

norwegian: Er det virkelig slik at vi ikke kan gjøre noe for å bremse klimaendringene?
english: Is it really the case that we cannot do anything to slow down climate change?
notes: Rhetorical yes/no question challenging a premise; 'virkelig slik at' intensifies

norwegian: Forskerteamet — bestående av eksperter fra fem land — presenterte sine funn på konferansen.
english: The research team — consisting of experts from five countries — presented their findings at the conference.
notes: Parenthetical with present participle phrase 'bestående av'

norwegian: Hvem kan si med sikkerhet hva fremtiden vil bringe for den neste generasjonen?
english: Who can say with certainty what the future holds for the next generation?
notes: Rhetorical question; indirect question 'hva fremtiden vil bringe' as object
accepted_answers: Who can say with certainty what the future has in store for the next generation

norwegian: Det er — og det har alltid vært — et grunnleggende menneskelig behov å tilhøre et fellesskap.
english: It is — and always has been — a fundamental human need to belong to a community.
notes: Double parenthetical with dashes; 'og det har alltid vært' = emphatic additive
accepted_answers: It is — and has always been — a fundamental human need to belong to a community

norwegian: Kan vi virkelig si at vi gjør nok for å ivareta de svakeste i samfunnet?
english: Can we really say that we are doing enough to protect the most vulnerable in society?
notes: Rhetorical question; 'virkelig' as intensifying adverb in yes/no question

norwegian: Løsningen — enkel i teorien, men kompleks i praksis — krever tett samarbeid mellom alle parter.
english: The solution — simple in theory, but complex in practice — requires close cooperation between all parties.
notes: Parenthetical as appositive contrast: 'enkel i teorien, men kompleks i praksis'
accepted_answers: The solution — simple in theory, but complex in practice — requires close collaboration between all parties

norwegian: Hva er egentlig meningen med et utdanningssystem som ikke forbereder elever på fremtidens utfordringer?
english: What is really the point of an education system that does not prepare students for the challenges of the future?
notes: Rhetorical wh-question; 'egentlig' as hedging intensifier

norwegian: Tallene — som ble publisert onsdag — viser at arbeidsledigheten har nådd sitt laveste nivå på ti år.
english: The figures — which were published on Wednesday — show that unemployment has reached its lowest level in ten years.
notes: Relative clause as dash-parenthetical; newspaper/formal register

norwegian: Hvorfor velger så mange unge å forlate bygdene når mulighetene er langt større i byene?
english: Why do so many young people choose to leave rural areas when the opportunities are far greater in the cities?
notes: Rhetorical why-question implying an obvious/unfortunate answer
accepted_answers: Why do so many young people choose to leave rural areas when opportunities are far greater in the cities

norwegian: Hvem — bortsett fra dem som tjener på systemet — vil egentlig forsvare status quo?
english: Who — apart from those who benefit from the system — would actually defend the status quo?
notes: Rhetorical question with parenthetical exception; 'egentlig' as challenge adverb
accepted_answers: Who — other than those who benefit from the system — would actually defend the status quo

norwegian: Direktøren — til alles overraskelse — trakk seg fra stillingen uten noen forklaring.
english: The director — to everyone's surprise — resigned from the position without any explanation.
notes: Prepositional phrase as dash-parenthetical; 'til alles overraskelse' — to everyone's surprise

norwegian: Er det ikke på tide at politikerne begynner å lytte til forskningsmiljøet i stedet for næringslivet?
english: Is it not about time that politicians started listening to the research community instead of the business sector?
notes: Rhetorical negated question: 'er det ikke på tide at' — aren't you overdue for

norwegian: Det norske velferdssamfunnet — engang ansett som et forbilde for verden — møter nå store utfordringer.
english: The Norwegian welfare society — once regarded as a model for the world — now faces major challenges.
notes: Participial phrase as parenthetical: 'engang ansett som' — once regarded as
accepted_answers: The Norwegian welfare state — once regarded as a model for the world — now faces major challenges

norwegian: Hva slags samfunn ønsker vi egentlig å leve i om femti år?
english: What kind of society do we actually want to live in in fifty years' time?
notes: 'hva slags' — what kind of; rhetorical wh-question with 'egentlig'

norwegian: Utfordringen — og det er en virkelig stor utfordring — er å finne løsninger som alle kan stille seg bak.
english: The challenge — and it is a truly great challenge — is to find solutions that everyone can get behind.
notes: Emphatic parenthetical that repeats and intensifies the subject
accepted_answers: The challenge — and it is a genuinely great challenge — is to find solutions that everyone can get behind

norwegian: Kan noen virkelig påstå at den nåværende politikken er tilstrekkelig for å møte klimakrisen?
english: Can anyone really claim that the current policy is sufficient to meet the climate crisis?
notes: Rhetorical modal question; 'virkelig' — challenge adverb
accepted_answers: Can anyone really argue that the current policy is sufficient to meet the climate crisis

norwegian: Mange vil kanskje ikke høre det, men sannheten er at vi ikke har råd til å vente lenger.
english: Many may not want to hear it, but the truth is that we cannot afford to wait any longer.
notes: Adversative construction; 'ikke ha råd til' = cannot afford to

norwegian: Hvem i denne salen vil stille seg opp og si at dette ikke er et alvorlig problem?
english: Who in this room will stand up and say that this is not a serious problem?
notes: Rhetorical challenge question; 'stille seg opp' — stand up (idiomatic)

norwegian: Beslutningen — tatt under stort press og i stor hast — viste seg å få alvorlige konsekvenser.
english: The decision — taken under great pressure and in great haste — proved to have serious consequences.
notes: Participial phrase as parenthetical: 'tatt under stort press og i stor hast'

norwegian: Er det mulig at vi har undervurdert omfanget av problemet helt fra starten av?
english: Is it possible that we have underestimated the scale of the problem from the very beginning?
notes: Self-critical rhetorical question; present perfect in embedded clause
accepted_answers: Is it possible that we have underestimated the extent of the problem from the very beginning
Could it be that we have underestimated the scale of the problem from the very beginning

norwegian: Fremtiden — usikker og uforutsigbar som den er — krever at vi tenker langsiktig.
english: The future — uncertain and unpredictable as it is — demands that we think long-term.
notes: Appositive concessive parenthetical: 'usikker og uforutsigbar som den er'
accepted_answers: The future — uncertain and unpredictable as it is — requires that we think long-term

norwegian: Hva betyr det egentlig å leve et godt liv i det moderne Norge?
english: What does it actually mean to live a good life in modern Norway?
notes: Philosophical rhetorical wh-question; 'egentlig' as probing adverb

norwegian: Initiativet — støttet av over hundre organisasjoner — har allerede samlet inn millioner til formålet.
english: The initiative — supported by over a hundred organisations — has already raised millions for the cause.
notes: Passive participial phrase as dash-parenthetical; 'samle inn' — raise (funds)
accepted_answers: The initiative — supported by over a hundred organizations — has already raised millions for the cause

norwegian: Vil vi som samfunn se tilbake på denne perioden med stolthet eller med skam?
english: Will we as a society look back on this period with pride or with shame?
notes: Rhetorical binary question; 'som samfunn' — appositive of subject 'vi'

norwegian: Innovasjonen — lenge betraktet som en utopi — er nå en realitet takket være ny teknologi.
english: The innovation — long regarded as a utopia — is now a reality thanks to new technology.
notes: Passive participial parenthetical with time adverb 'lenge'

norwegian: Er det ikke nettopp i de vanskeligste tidene at vi viser hvem vi egentlig er?
english: Is it not precisely in the most difficult times that we show who we really are?
notes: Rhetorical cleft question: 'er det ikke nettopp i ... at'; 'egentlig' as deepening adverb
accepted_answers: Is it not exactly in the most difficult times that we show who we really are

norwegian: Viser til samtalen vår fra mandag og ønsker å bekrefte de avtalte punktene skriftlig.
english: With reference to our conversation from Monday, I wish to confirm the agreed points in writing.
notes: 'viser til' — standard formal email opener; infinitive subject (implicit 'jeg') is acceptable in formal writing

norwegian: Vedlagt finner De vår offisielle tilbudsbekreftelse med de gjeldende betingelsene.
english: Enclosed you will find our official order confirmation with the applicable terms.
notes: 'vedlagt finner De' — V2 inversion after fronted participle; 'gjeldende' = applicable/current
accepted_answers: Enclosed please find our official order confirmation with the applicable terms
Enclosed you will find our official offer confirmation with the applicable terms

norwegian: Jeg tillater meg å videresende denne forespørselen til ___ avdeling for videre behandling.
english: I take the liberty of forwarding this enquiry to the relevant department for further processing.
notes: ansvarlig
accepted_answers: kompetent

norwegian: Vi bekrefter herved mottak av Deres søknad og vil komme tilbake med svar innen fem virkedager.
english: We hereby confirm receipt of your application and will get back to you with an answer within five working days.
notes: 'bekrefter herved mottak av' — formal acknowledgement phrase; 'Deres' formal address
accepted_answers: We hereby confirm receipt of your application and will respond within five working days
We hereby confirm receipt of your application and will get back to you within five working days

norwegian: På vegne av styret ønsker jeg å ønske alle ansatte en god og ___ ferie.
english: On behalf of the board, I wish to wish all employees a good and well-deserved holiday.
notes: velfortjent

norwegian: I henhold til kontraktens punkt 4.2 er leverandøren forpliktet til å varsle om forsinkelser innen 48 timer.
english: In accordance with clause 4.2 of the contract, the supplier is obliged to notify of delays within 48 hours.
notes: 'i henhold til' — in accordance with (formal); 'forpliktet til å' = obliged to
accepted_answers: In accordance with clause 4.2 of the contract, the supplier is obligated to notify of delays within 48 hours

norwegian: Vi ber om at eventuelle bemerkninger til referat fra møtet sendes til undertegnede innen fredag.
english: We ask that any comments on the minutes of the meeting be sent to the undersigned by Friday.
notes: 'undertegnede' — the undersigned (formal self-reference); 'referat fra møtet' = meeting minutes

norwegian: Saken er nå oversendt til juridisk avdeling for ___ og vi vil holde Dem orientert om fremdriften.
english: The matter has now been referred to the legal department for review and we will keep you informed of progress.
notes: gjennomgang
accepted_answers: vurdering

norwegian: Det vises til tidligere korrespondanse i saken, og vi ønsker å presisere vår posisjon.
english: Reference is made to earlier correspondence in the matter, and we wish to clarify our position.
notes: 'det vises til' — impersonal passive reference opener; 'presisere' = clarify/specify
accepted_answers: We refer to earlier correspondence in the matter, and we wish to clarify our position

norwegian: Jeg tillater meg å henstille til avdelingslederne om å videreformidle denne informasjonen til sine medarbeidere.
english: I take the liberty of requesting the department heads to pass this information on to their colleagues.
notes: 'henstille til' — formally request/urge; 'videreformidle' = pass on/convey
accepted_answers: I take the liberty of asking the department heads to pass this information on to their colleagues

norwegian: Takk for godt møte tidligere i dag. Her følger en oppsummering av de viktigste ___ .
english: Thank you for a productive meeting earlier today. Here follows a summary of the key action points.
notes: oppfølgingspunktene
accepted_answers: tiltakspunktene

norwegian: Med referanse til telefonsamtalen av dags dato ønsker vi å formalisere avtalen skriftlig.
english: With reference to the telephone conversation of today's date, we wish to formalise the agreement in writing.
notes: 'dags dato' — formal expression for today's date; 'formalisere skriftlig' = formalise in writing

norwegian: Selskapet beklager eventuelle ulemper dette måtte ha medført og takker for Deres forståelse.
english: The company apologises for any inconvenience this may have caused and thanks you for your understanding.
notes: 'eventuelle ulemper dette måtte ha medført' — formal hedged apology; 'Deres forståelse' = formal address
accepted_answers: The company apologizes for any inconvenience this may have caused and thanks you for your understanding

norwegian: Vedlegg: ___ , pristilbud datert 20. mai og signert samarbeidsavtale.
english: Attachments: Project description, price quote dated 20 May and signed cooperation agreement.
notes: Prosjektbeskrivelse

norwegian: Vi setter stor pris på det gode samarbeidet vi har hatt og ser frem til å fortsette dette fremover.
english: We greatly appreciate the good cooperation we have had and look forward to continuing this going forward.
notes: 'setter stor pris på' — formal appreciation; 'fremover' = going forward/in the future

norwegian: Det fremgår av vedlagte dokumentasjon at fristen for innlevering ble overholdt.
english: It is evident from the attached documentation that the submission deadline was met.
notes: 'det fremgår av' — it is evident/clear from (formal evidential); 'vedlagte' = attached
accepted_answers: It is clear from the attached documentation that the submission deadline was met

norwegian: Agendaen for morgendagens møte er som følger: 1) Status ___ , 2) Budsjettgjennomgang, 3) Eventuelt.
english: The agenda for tomorrow's meeting is as follows: 1) Project status, 2) Budget review, 3) Any other business.
notes: prosjekt

norwegian: Dersom De har ytterligere spørsmål, er De hjertelig velkommen til å ta kontakt med oss.
english: Should you have any further questions, you are most welcome to contact us.
notes: 'dersom De har' — formal conditional; 'hjertelig velkommen' = most welcome (formal)
accepted_answers: Should you have any additional questions, you are most welcome to contact us
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us

norwegian: Referat fra styremøtet den 12. juni vil bli distribuert til alle ___ innen utgangen av denne uken.
english: The minutes from the board meeting on 12 June will be distributed to all board members by the end of this week.
notes: styremedlemmene

norwegian: Vi tillater oss å minne om at betalingsfristen utløper den 30. juni og ber om snarlig oppgjør.
english: We take the liberty of reminding you that the payment deadline expires on 30 June and ask for prompt settlement.
notes: 'tillater oss å minne om' — formal reminder phrasing; 'snarlig oppgjør' = prompt settlement
accepted_answers: We take the liberty of reminding you that the payment deadline expires on 30 June and request prompt settlement

norwegian: Med forbehold om godkjenning fra styret vil prosjektet ___ i løpet av tredje kvartal.
english: Subject to board approval, the project will be launched during the third quarter.
notes: lanseres
accepted_answers: igangsettes

norwegian: Vi ber om at dette behandles konfidensielt inntil en offisiell kunngjøring er foretatt.
english: We ask that this be treated confidentially until an official announcement has been made.
notes: 'behandles konfidensielt' — s-passive; 'foretatt' = made/carried out (formal)

norwegian: Som avtalt i møtet vil jeg sende over en revidert versjon av forslaget ___ .
english: As agreed in the meeting, I will send over a revised version of the proposal by the end of the day.
notes: innen dagen er omme
accepted_answers: innen arbeidstidens slutt

norwegian: Det bekreftes at ovennevnte person er fast ansatt i selskapet fra og med 1. mars i år.
english: It is hereby confirmed that the above-mentioned person has been permanently employed by the company as of 1 March this year.
notes: 'ovennevnte' — above-mentioned (formal bureaucratic); 'fra og med' = as of/from
accepted_answers: It is hereby confirmed that the above-mentioned person has been permanently employed by the company from 1 March this year

norwegian: Vi viser til Deres henvendelse og beklager at vi ikke har kunnet besvare den ___ .
english: We refer to your enquiry and apologise that we have not been able to respond to it sooner.
notes: tidligere
accepted_answers: raskere

norwegian: Etter en grundig gjennomgang av Deres søknad har vi dessverre kommet til at stillingen er besatt.
english: After a thorough review of your application, we regret to inform you that the position has been filled.
notes: 'dessverre kommet til at' — formal regret phrase; 'stillingen er besatt' = position is filled
accepted_answers: After a careful review of your application, we regret to inform you that the position has been filled

norwegian: Jeg setter stor pris på muligheten til å presentere oss og håper vi kan ___ et møte.
english: I greatly appreciate the opportunity to present ourselves and hope we can arrange a meeting.
notes: avtale
accepted_answers: arrangere

norwegian: Høfligst ber vi om at De returnerer det undertegnede skjemaet til oss innen den oppgitte fristen.
english: We kindly ask that you return the signed form to us by the stated deadline.
notes: 'høfligst ber vi om' — most politely request (formal polite request); 'De' formal address

norwegian: Vi ønsker å informere Dem om at den planlagte nedstengningen vil finne sted den 15. august fra kl. 22.00.
english: We wish to inform you that the planned shutdown will take place on 15 August from 22:00.
notes: 'ønsker å informere Dem om at' — formal notification opening; 'Dem' formal address
accepted_answers: We would like to inform you that the planned shutdown will take place on 15 August from 22:00

norwegian: Avslutningsvis ønsker vi å takke for et konstruktivt møte og ser frem til videre samarbeid.
english: In closing, we wish to thank you for a constructive meeting and look forward to further cooperation.
notes: 'avslutningsvis' — in closing/to conclude (formal adverb); V2 inversion after fronting

norwegian: Selv om resultatene var lovende, er det for tidlig å trekke sikre konklusjoner.
english: Although the results were promising, it is too early to draw firm conclusions.
notes: Concessive 'selv om'-clause fronted for rhetorical effect; same meaning as 'resultatene var lovende, men det er for tidlig...' — teaches how fronting shifts stylistic weight

norwegian: Ikke bare er prisen høy, men kvaliteten er heller ikke det man kunne forvente.
english: Not only is the price high, but the quality is not what one might expect either.
notes: Fronted negative 'ikke bare' triggers inversion; stylistic contrast with a neutral SVO equivalent

norwegian: Det som overrasket oss mest, var at deltakerne svarte så raskt.
english: What surprised us most was that the participants responded so quickly.
notes: Cleft construction 'det som ... var at' — fronts the point of surprise; compare plain 'vi ble mest overrasket over at...'

norwegian: Hadde vi hatt mer tid, ville vi ha gjennomført en grundigere analyse.
english: Had we had more time, we would have carried out a more thorough analysis.
notes: Inverted conditional without 'hvis' — formal/literary register; contrast with 'hvis vi hadde hatt mer tid'

norwegian: Rapporten er skrevet på en klar og konsis måte, noe som gjør den lett tilgjengelig.
english: The report is written in a clear and concise way, which makes it easily accessible.
notes: 'noe som' as a non-restrictive relative pronoun referring to the whole preceding clause — a stylistic device absent in everyday speech
accepted_answers: The report is written in a clear and concise manner, which makes it easily accessible

norwegian: Det er en ting å love endringer; en helt annen ting er å faktisk gjennomføre dem.
english: It is one thing to promise changes; quite another thing is to actually implement them.
notes: Rhetorical antithesis using ellipsis in the second clause; stylistically stronger than 'det er vanskelig å gjennomføre det man lover'

norwegian: Tiltaket har hatt en positiv effekt — om enn begrenset — på arbeidsledigheten i regionen.
english: The measure has had a positive effect — albeit limited — on unemployment in the region.
notes: Parenthetical concession 'om enn' (albeit) inserted mid-sentence — a formal stylistic hedge; contrast with a separate concessive clause
accepted_answers: The measure has had a positive impact — albeit limited — on unemployment in the region

norwegian: Snarere enn å avvise forslaget, burde man vurdere det grundig.
english: Rather than rejecting the proposal, one should consider it thoroughly.
notes: 'snarere enn å' (rather than + infinitive) — formal contrastive framing; inversion after fronted adverbial

norwegian: Problemet er ikke mangel på ressurser, men snarere mangel på vilje til å bruke dem.
english: The problem is not a lack of resources, but rather a lack of willingness to use them.
notes: 'ikke ... men snarere' (not ... but rather) — stylistic correction construction that redirects the reader's frame

norwegian: Så vel ledelsen som de ansatte var enige om at endringen var nødvendig.
english: Both management and employees agreed that the change was necessary.
notes: 'så vel ... som' (both ... and) — formal correlative; more elevated register than 'både ... og'

norwegian: Hva angår budsjettet, ___ det nødvendig med ytterligere diskusjon.
english: As for the budget, further discussion is necessary.
notes: er

norwegian: Nettopp fordi situasjonen er kompleks, krever den en nyansert tilnærming.
english: Precisely because the situation is complex, it requires a nuanced approach.
notes: Fronted causal clause with emphatic 'nettopp' — intensifies the logical connection; stylistically more forceful than 'siden situasjonen er kompleks'

norwegian: Langt fra å løse problemet, har tiltaket skapt nye utfordringer.
english: Far from solving the problem, the measure has created new challenges.
notes: 'langt fra å' (far from + infinitive) — formal adversative construction; inversion after fronted phrase

norwegian: Det er ikke alltid at den raskeste løsningen er den beste.
english: It is not always the case that the quickest solution is the best one.
notes: Cleft-like construction 'det er ikke alltid at' — formal generalisation device; stylistically heavier than 'den raskeste løsningen er ikke alltid den beste'

norwegian: Uavhengig av utfallet vil erfaringene vi har gjort, være verdifulle for fremtidige prosjekter.
english: Regardless of the outcome, the experience we have gained will be valuable for future projects.
notes: 'uavhengig av' (regardless of) — formal fronted preposition phrase; embedded relative clause 'vi har gjort' with zero relative pronoun
accepted_answers: Regardless of the outcome, the experience we have acquired will be valuable for future projects

norwegian: Enten man liker det eller ikke, er globalisering et faktum vi må forholde oss til.
english: Whether one likes it or not, globalisation is a fact we must relate to.
notes: Concessive 'enten ... eller ikke' fronted with V2; stylistic alternative to 'selv om mange ikke liker det'

norwegian: Til tross for at rapporten ble levert i tide, ble konklusjonene møtt med skepsis.
english: Despite the report being delivered on time, the conclusions were met with scepticism.
notes: 'til tross for at' (despite the fact that) — formal concessive; contrast with informal 'selv om'

norwegian: Det er nettopp i møtet mellom ulike perspektiver at de beste ideene oppstår.
english: It is precisely in the meeting of different perspectives that the best ideas arise.
notes: Full cleft 'det er ... at' with a prepositional phrase as the focus; literary/formal register
accepted_answers: It is exactly in the meeting of different perspectives that the best ideas arise

norwegian: Verken mangel på penger eller mangel på tid kan forklare den svake fremgangen.
english: Neither a lack of money nor a lack of time can explain the weak progress.
notes: Fronted 'verken ... eller' (neither ... nor) — formal double negation; inversion after fronted negative subject

norwegian: Jeg er litt usikker på om forslaget egentlig holder mål, for å si det ærlig.
english: I am a bit unsure whether the proposal really holds up, to be honest.
notes: Informal hedging: 'litt usikker på om', 'for å si det ærlig' — contrast with formal 'det kan stilles spørsmål ved om forslaget er tilstrekkelig'

norwegian: Vi tillater oss å bemerke at de avtalte fristene ikke er overholdt.
english: We allow ourselves to note that the agreed deadlines have not been met.
notes: 'tillater oss å bemerke' — highly formal, almost legalistic register for raising a complaint; contrast with informal 'vi synes dere ikke har holdt fristene'
accepted_answers: We take the liberty of noting that the agreed deadlines have not been met
We wish to note that the agreed deadlines have not been met

norwegian: Mellom oss sagt er det ingen som tror at dette prosjektet vil lykkes.
english: Between us, nobody really believes this project will succeed.
notes: 'mellom oss sagt' — colloquial confidence-marker (between you and me); signals informal register shift; V2 after fronted phrase

norwegian: Departementet ___ til at saken vil bli behandlet innen utgangen av kvartalet.
english: The ministry points to the matter being handled by the end of the quarter.
notes: viser

norwegian: Det er ikke akkurat slik vi hadde sett for oss at samarbeidet ville utvikle seg.
english: This is not quite how we had envisioned the collaboration would develop.
notes: Understated informal criticism; utvikle seg (develop) is the natural verb; contrast with formal samarbeidet har ikke innfridd forventningene
accepted_answers: This is not exactly how we had envisioned the collaboration would develop
This is not quite how we had imagined the collaboration would develop

norwegian: Det fremkommer av dokumentasjonen at selskapet ikke har fulgt gjeldende regelverk.
english: It emerges from the documentation that the company has not followed the applicable regulations.
notes: 'det fremkommer av' — formal/official register for 'it emerges from'; 'gjeldende regelverk' (applicable regulations) is bureaucratic vocabulary

norwegian: Jeg skjønner hva du mener, men jeg er ikke helt enig i konklusjonen din.
english: I understand what you mean, but I do not entirely agree with your conclusion.
notes: 'ikke helt enig' (not entirely in agreement) — the partial negation softens the disagreement; informal spoken register; contrast with formal 'vi finner ikke grunnlag for å slutte oss til konklusjonen'

norwegian: Det er en sak for styret å avgjøre, og vi ber om at den behandles i henhold til vedtektene.
english: This is a matter for the board to decide, and we request that it be handled in accordance with the statutes.
notes: 'ber om at' + subjunctive-like construction; 'i henhold til vedtektene' — formal legal register; note the tonal distance from everyday speech
accepted_answers: This is a matter for the board to decide, and we ask that it be handled in accordance with the statutes
This is a matter for the board to decide, and we request that it be handled in accordance with the bylaws

norwegian: Æsj, jeg visste jo at det kom til å gå galt — det var bare et tidsspørsmål.
english: Ugh, I knew it was going to go wrong — it was only a matter of time.
notes: Colloquial register: discourse particle 'æsj', epistemic 'jo' (as you'd expect); 'et tidsspørsmål' — informal spoken Norwegian

norwegian: Jeg tillater meg å stille spørsmål ved om de fremlagte tallene er pålitelige.
english: I allow myself to question whether the presented figures are reliable.
notes: 'tillater meg å stille spørsmål ved om' — extremely formal critical register; the indirectness is the register signal; contrast with blunt 'jeg tror ikke tallene stemmer'

norwegian: Kollegaen min er flink nok, men han har en tendens til å ___ ting unødvendig.
english: My colleague is capable enough, but he has a tendency to overcomplicate things.
notes: komplisere

norwegian: Herved bekreftes det at søknaden er mottatt og vil bli behandlet innen tre virkedager.
english: It is hereby confirmed that the application has been received and will be processed within three working days.
notes: 'herved bekreftes det at' — archaic/official formula used in formal correspondence and receipts; entirely absent from informal register
accepted_answers: We hereby confirm that the application has been received and will be processed within three working days

norwegian: Han snakket i vei om alt mulig, uten å egentlig si noe som helst av substans.
english: He talked on and on about everything, without actually saying anything of substance.
notes: 'snakke i vei' (talk on and on) — informal idiomatic phrase; 'alt mulig' and 'noe som helst' reinforce informal register
accepted_answers: He went on and on about everything, without actually saying anything of substance

norwegian: Partene er blitt enige om at tvisten skal løses ved voldgift fremfor domstolsbehandling.
english: The parties have agreed that the dispute shall be resolved by arbitration rather than court proceedings.
notes: 'er blitt enige om at' + 'fremfor' — formal contractual register; 'voldgift' and 'domstolsbehandling' are technical legal vocabulary

norwegian: Å si det rett ut: planene holder ikke vann, og det vet alle i rommet.
english: To put it plainly: the plans do not hold water, and everyone in the room knows it.
notes: 'å si det rett ut' — meta-discourse marker announcing a register shift to blunt directness; 'holde vann' is a semi-formal idiom

norwegian: I den grad det er mulig, bør man unngå å trekke forhastede konklusjoner.
english: To the extent that it is possible, one should avoid drawing hasty conclusions.
notes: 'i den grad det er mulig' — formal hedging quantifier phrase; V2 after fronted clause; contrast with informal 'hvis det er mulig'

norwegian: Det var egentlig et ganske kjedelig møte, men sjefen virket fornøyd, så.
english: It was actually quite a boring meeting, but the boss seemed satisfied, so.
notes: Spoken informal: trailing så particle; neuter møte needs article et
accepted_answers: It was actually quite a dull meeting, but the boss seemed satisfied, so
It was actually rather a boring meeting, but the boss seemed satisfied, so

norwegian: Saksbehandlingstiden ___ ikke overstige seks uker fra søknadsdato.
english: The processing time shall not exceed six weeks from the date of application.
notes: skal

norwegian: Det er jo ikke verdens undergang om vi ikke rekker fristen, da.
english: It is not the end of the world if we do not make the deadline, you know.
notes: Informal oral register: discourse particles 'jo' and 'da'; hyperbole 'verdens undergang'; contrast with formal deadline correspondence

norwegian: Klagen er fremsatt innen fristen og vil bli undergitt en grundig saksbehandling.
english: The complaint has been lodged within the deadline and will be subjected to a thorough case review.
notes: 'fremsatt' (lodged/submitted) and 'undergitt en grundig saksbehandling' — formal administrative language found in official responses to complaints
accepted_answers: The complaint has been filed within the deadline and will be subjected to a thorough case review
The complaint has been submitted within the deadline and will be subjected to a thorough case review

norwegian: Vi ber om at tilbudet bekreftes skriftlig innen fredag den femte.
english: We request that the offer be confirmed in writing by Friday the fifth.
notes: 'ber om at ... bekreftes' — standard business correspondence formula; s-passive in subordinate clause

norwegian: Stillingen krever dokumentert erfaring fra prosjektledelse og gode kommunikasjonsevner.
english: The position requires documented experience in project management and strong communication skills.
notes: Job advertisement register: 'dokumentert erfaring fra' + noun phrase; compound 'kommunikasjonsevner' — core professional vocabulary

norwegian: Vedlagt finner du vår oppdaterte prisliste samt de generelle leveringsbetingelsene.
english: Enclosed you will find our updated price list as well as the general delivery terms.
notes: 'vedlagt finner du' — fronted participial phrase triggers inversion; standard email/letter opener in commercial correspondence

norwegian: Styret har vedtatt å iverksette en ekstern gjennomgang av selskapets økonomi.
english: The board has resolved to initiate an external review of the company's finances.
notes: 'vedtatt å iverksette' (resolved to initiate) — boardroom register; 'ekstern gjennomgang' (external review) is standard corporate governance vocabulary
accepted_answers: The board has decided to initiate an external review of the company's finances

norwegian: Vi viser til din e-post av 28. mai og ___ den sene tilbakemeldingen.
english: We refer to your email of 28 May and apologise for the late response.
notes: beklager

norwegian: Søkeren må dokumentere at vedkommende behersker norsk på minimum B2-nivå.
english: The applicant must document that they command Norwegian at a minimum B2 level.
notes: 'vedkommende' — formal gender-neutral third person pronoun used in official documents; 'beherske' (to command/master) is a professional register verb
accepted_answers: The applicant must demonstrate that they command Norwegian at a minimum B2 level

norwegian: Etter en grundig vurdering har vi besluttet å ikke gå videre med din søknad på dette tidspunktet.
english: After a thorough assessment, we have decided not to proceed with your application at this time.
notes: 'etter en grundig vurdering' — formal rejection letter opener; 'besluttet å ikke' vs 'ikke besluttet å' — placement of negation with infinitive

norwegian: Alle ansatte ___ om å gjøre seg kjent med den nye personalpolitikken innen 1. juli.
english: All employees are asked to familiarise themselves with the new personnel policy by 1 July.
notes: anmodes

norwegian: Konferansen samler ledende eksperter innen bærekraft, innovasjon og digital transformasjon.
english: The conference brings together leading experts in sustainability, innovation, and digital transformation.
notes: 'samler ledende eksperter innen' — professional event description register; 'innen' (within/in the field of) vs 'i' — preposition choice in professional contexts

norwegian: Det er et krav at alle tilbydere leverer et fullstendig anbud med relevante referanser.
english: It is a requirement that all tenderers submit a complete bid with relevant references.
notes: 'tilbydere' (tenderers) and 'anbud' (tender/bid) are procurement vocabulary; 'det er et krav at' — formal obligation construction
accepted_answers: It is a requirement that all bidders submit a complete bid with relevant references

norwegian: Rapporten skal overleveres til oppdragsgiver senest to uker etter at feltarbeidet er avsluttet.
english: The report shall be delivered to the client no later than two weeks after the fieldwork has been completed.
notes: 'oppdragsgiver' (client/principal) — project management vocabulary; 'senest' (no later than) and 'overleveres' (be delivered) are formal contractual terms

norwegian: Vi tar sikte på å ferdigstille prosjektet innen avtalt tidsfrist og innenfor det oppsatte budsjettet.
english: We aim to complete the project within the agreed deadline and within the set budget.
notes: 'tar sikte på å' (aim to) — standard professional goal statement; 'ferdigstille' vs 'fullføre' — register distinction; 'oppsatt budsjett' (set/established budget)

norwegian: Kandidater med relevant mastergrad vil bli foretrukket, men det er ikke et absolutt krav.
english: Candidates with a relevant master's degree will be preferred, but it is not an absolute requirement.
notes: 'vil bli foretrukket' — future passive in job advertisement register; hedging 'men det er ikke et absolutt krav' is a standard qualification softener

norwegian: Eventuelle innsigelser mot vedtaket må fremsettes skriftlig innen klagefristen på tre uker.
english: Any objections to the decision must be submitted in writing within the three-week appeal period.
notes: 'eventuelle innsigelser' (any objections) — formal legal/administrative vocabulary; 'klagefrist' (appeal deadline) is a key legal term; 'fremsettes skriftlig' — formal passive imperative
accepted_answers: Any objections to the decision must be filed in writing within the three-week appeal period

norwegian: Tilbudet er gyldig i 30 dager fra tilbudsdato, med mindre annet er avtalt skriftlig.
english: The offer is valid for 30 days from the date of the offer, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
notes: 'med mindre annet er avtalt skriftlig' — standard commercial clause; 'tilbudsdato' (date of offer) is a commerce compound; tests ability to produce formal contract language

norwegian: Partene er enige om at tvister som oppstår i tilknytning til denne avtalen, skal behandles etter norsk rett.
english: The parties agree that disputes arising in connection with this agreement shall be governed by Norwegian law.
notes: 'i tilknytning til' (in connection with) — formal legal prepositional phrase; 'etter norsk rett' (under Norwegian law) is standard contract language
accepted_answers: The parties agree that disputes arising under this agreement shall be governed by Norwegian law

norwegian: Vi setter stor pris på det gode samarbeidet og ser frem til å videreføre dette i det kommende året.
english: We greatly appreciate the good collaboration and look forward to continuing it in the coming year.
notes: 'setter stor pris på' + 'ser frem til å' — paired professional courtesy phrases used in partnership correspondence and year-end communications

norwegian: Avdelingsleder er ansvarlig for at alle medarbeidere gjennomfører det obligatoriske sikkerhetskurset.
english: The department manager is responsible for ensuring that all staff complete the mandatory safety course.
notes: 'ansvarlig for at' + subordinate clause — formal management responsibility construction; 'medarbeidere' (staff/co-workers) is the neutral professional term vs 'ansatte'

norwegian: Arbeidsgiver forbeholder seg retten til å endre arbeidsoppgaver innenfor rammen av stillingsbeskrivelsen.
english: The employer reserves the right to change work tasks within the scope of the job description.
notes: 'forbeholder seg retten til å' (reserves the right to) — standard employment contract formula; 'innenfor rammen av' (within the scope/framework of) is formal register
accepted_answers: The employer reserves the right to modify work tasks within the scope of the job description
The employer reserves the right to change duties within the scope of the job description

norwegian: Huset brant ned til grunnen fordi noen hadde glemt å slukke stearinlyset.
english: The house burned down to the ground because someone had forgotten to blow out the candle.
notes: Plain neutral register baseline — no stylistic elevation. Compare with formal variant using 'nedbrente' or literary inversion.

norwegian: Ned til grunnen brente huset — ikke én murstein stod igjen.
english: Down to the ground the house burned — not a single brick remained.
notes: Literary/rhetorical register: fronted adverbial for dramatic effect + short asyndetic follow-up clause. V2 inversion required after fronted element.

norwegian: Møtet ble avholdt i henhold til gjeldende prosedyrer og uten nevneverdige avvik.
english: The meeting was held in accordance with current procedures and without noteworthy deviations.
notes: Bureaucratic/administrative register: 'avholdt', 'i henhold til', 'gjeldende', 'nevneverdige avvik' are formal register markers.

norwegian: Vi fikk beskjed om at møtet var ferdig, og at alt gikk bra.
english: We were told that the meeting was over and that everything went well.
notes: Neutral conversational register baseline. Contrast with the formal version b2-sv2-203 — same event, very different register.

norwegian: Stille lå fjorden da solen gikk ned bak fjellene.
english: Still lay the fjord as the sun went down behind the mountains.
notes: Poetic/literary register: fronted predicate adjective 'stille' triggers subject-verb inversion — a hallmark of literary Bokmål style.

norwegian: Fjorden var stille da solen gikk ned bak fjellene.
english: The fjord was still as the sun went down behind the mountains.
notes: Neutral register version of b2-sv2-205. Gap version: 'Fjorden var _____ da solen gikk ned bak fjellene.' Expected answer: stille

norwegian: Da vi kom hjem, var pengene allerede ___ brukt, og ingenting var igjen av arven.
english: When we came home, the money was already spent, and nothing remained of the inheritance.
notes: var

norwegian: Borte var pengene, og ingenting var igjen av arven.
english: Gone was the money, and nothing remained of the inheritance.
notes: Literary register: fronted predicative adjective 'borte' triggers inversion 'var pengene'. Same content as gap b2-sv2-207 but rhetorically elevated.

norwegian: Det skal bemerkes at rapporten ikke dekker perioden etter 2020.
english: It should be noted that the report does not cover the period after 2020.
notes: Academic/formal register: impersonal 'det skal bemerkes at' — a register-specific hedging formula. Contrast with spoken 'jeg vil bare si at rapporten ikke...'

norwegian: Barnet gråt. Ingenting hjalp — ikke sang, ikke kos, ikke mat.
english: The child wept. Nothing helped — not singing, not cuddling, not food.
notes: Narrative literary register: short staccato opening sentence + tricolon with anaphoric 'ikke'. This rhetorical patterning marks literary prose style. Note: 'ingenting' is always one word in Bokmål; 'verken ... verken ... verken' is not a standard Bokmål correlative pattern — use 'ikke ... ikke ... ikke' for anaphoraic negation across a list.

norwegian: Vi ber om at kunden ___ seg til nærmeste filial for videre veiledning.
english: We ask that the customer contact the nearest branch for further guidance.
notes: henvender

norwegian: Sakte, sakte steg solen over horisonten og farget himmelen i rosa og gull.
english: Slowly, slowly the sun rose over the horizon and coloured the sky in pink and gold.
notes: Literary register: doubled adverb 'sakte, sakte' for rhythmic effect + V2 inversion. The repetition is a deliberate stylistic device, not an error.

norwegian: Selskapet har til dags dato ikke mottatt noen skriftlig bekreftelse på ordren.
english: The company has to date not received any written confirmation of the order.
notes: 'til dags dato' — formal temporal phrase (to date/as of today); 'skriftlig bekreftelse' — formal noun phrase. Register markers throughout.

norwegian: Vinden tok tak i håret hennes og ___ det ut over skuldrene som et vilt flagg.
english: The wind caught her hair and spread it across her shoulders like a wild flag.
notes: spredte

norwegian: Ovennevnte forhold tilsier at kontrakten bør reforhandles innen utgangen av inneværende kvartal.
english: The above-mentioned circumstances suggest that the contract should be renegotiated by the end of the current quarter.
notes: 'Ovennevnte' (above-mentioned), 'tilsier' (suggest/indicate), 'inneværende kvartal' (current quarter) — all high-register administrative markers.

norwegian: Han hadde alltid lurt på hva som lå bak det stille smilet hennes.
english: He had always wondered what lay behind her quiet smile.
notes: Neutral narrative register — plain prose, no rhetorical elevation. Good baseline for comparing against literary variants.

norwegian: Hvem var hun egentlig, denne kvinnen med det stille smilet som aldri rakk øynene?
